messages.content: Return only valid JSON that matches the requested schema. Do not include markdown or explanations.

Stage 2: Heading Classification
Return JSON only.
Classify every detected heading or content block into one of:
- unit
- chapter
- topic
- subtopic
- formative_content
- practical
- project
- activity
- assessment
- teacher_note
- excluded_content
- unknown

Rules:
- A valid chapter title should usually be short and concept-like
- If a detected chapter is long, sentence-like, has semicolons, many commas, or many clauses, reclassify it as topic content
- If the phrase before a colon is short and the text after it is explanatory, treat the short phrase as heading and the rest as topic content
- Do not hardcode subject-specific logic

Raw extraction:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Science",
    "class": "IX and X",
    "grade": "9, 10",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2025-26",
    "total_pages": "13"
  },
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "IX",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination (80 Marks)",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Materials",
            "Unit I: Matter-Nature and Behaviour"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Matter in Our Surroundings",
            "Is Matter Around Us Pure",
            "Atoms and Molecules",
            "Structure of atom"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter (solid, liquid, gas); change of state (melting, freezing, evaporation, condensation, sublimation)",
            "Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions",
            "Physical and chemical changes",
            "Atoms and molecules; Law of Chemical Combination; Chemical formulae; Atomic and molecular masses",
            "Sub-atomic particles (Electrons, protons, neutrons); Models of atom; Valency; Atomic Number and Mass Number; Isotopes and Isobars"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Absorption of heat in melting; cooling by evaporation",
            "Pure and Impure substances",
            "Excluding separating the components of a mixture"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Organization in the Living World",
          "marks": 22,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: The World of the Living",
            "Unit II: Organization in the Living World"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Cell - Basic Unit of life",
            "Tissues, Organs, Organ System, Organism"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; multicellular organisms",
            "Cell membrane and cell wall; cell organelles (chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus); nucleus; chromosomes",
            "Structure and functions of animal tissues (four types) and plant tissues (Meristematic and Permanent)",
            "Organs, Organ System, Organism"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Basic structure and number of chromosomes"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Motion, Force and Work",
          "marks": 27,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Moving Things, People and Ideas",
            "Unit III: Motion, Force and Work"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Motion",
            "Force and Newton's laws",
            "Gravitation",
            "Floatation",
            "Work, Energy and Power",
            "Sound"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Distance and displacement; velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion; acceleration; distance-time and velocity-time graphs; elementary idea of uniform circular motion",
            "Force and Motion; Newton's Laws of Motion; Action and Reaction forces; Inertia (of a body, mass); Momentum; Force and Acceleration",
            "Universal Law of Gravitation; Gravity; Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall",
            "Thrust and Pressure; Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy",
            "Work done by a Force; Energy (Kinetic, Potential); Power; Conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit)",
            "Nature of sound; propagation in media; speed of sound; range of hearing; ultrasound; reflection; echo"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Excluding separating components",
            "Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum (Formative only)"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Food Production",
          "marks": 6,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Food",
            "Unit IV: Food Production"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Plant and animal breeding",
            "Fertilizers and manures",
            "Protection from pests",
            "Organic farming"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Breeding and selection for quality improvement",
            "Use of fertilizers and manures",
            "Protection from pests and diseases",
            "Organic farming"
          ],
          "subtopics": []
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "X",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination (80 Marks)",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,

            "Properties of metals/non-metals; Reactivity series; Ionic compounds; Metallurgical processes; Corrosion prevention",
            "Covalent bonds; Versatile nature of carbon; Hydrocarbons (saturated/unsaturated); Homologous series; Nomenclature; Chemical properties (combustion, oxidation, addition, substitution); Ethanol and Ethanoic acid; Soaps and detergents"

          "subtopics": [
            "Definition relating to logarithm not required for pH",
            "Only properties and uses of ethanol/ethanoic acid"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "World of Living",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [

            "Control and co-ordination in animals and plants",
            "Reproduction",
            "Heredity and Evolution"

            "Nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals",
            "Tropic movements; plant hormones; Nervous system (Voluntary, involuntary, reflex); Chemical co-ordination (animal hormones)",
            "Reproduction in animals/plants (asexual/sexual); reproductive health; family planning methods; Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS; Child bearing/women's health",
            "Heredity; Mendel's contribution/Laws of inheritance; Sex determination"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Brief introduction to evolution concepts implied in heredity section"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Natural Phenomena",

            "Unit III: Natural Phenomena"

          "possible_chapters": [

            "Refraction and Lenses",
            "Human Eye and Dispersion"

          "topics": [
            "Spherical mirrors (centre, axis, focus, focal length); Mirror formula; Magnification",
            "Laws of refraction; Refractive index; Spherical lenses; Lens formula; Power of a lens",
            "Functioning of human eye; defects and corrections; Applications of spherical mirrors/lenses",
            "Refraction through prism; dispersion; scattering"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Derivation not required for formulas",
            "Excluding colour of the sun at sunrise/sunset"

        },

          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Effects of Current",

            "Heating effect applications; Electric power (P, V, I relation)",
            "Magnetic field and lines; Field due to conductor/coil/solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor; Fleming's Left Hand Rule",
            "Direct Current vs Alternating Current (frequency); Advantage of AC over DC"

      "title": "Preparation of solutions (Class IX)",
